pathAssistantSteps.info:
Intake and Screening

  • Confirm applicant contact, project description, and action type are complete.
  • Run the CE Intake OmniScript if reviewing for Categorical Exclusion.
  • Verify GIS proximity results have run — check for any [endpoint unavailable] flags.
  • If EJ or tribal proximity triggered, confirm the EJ/Tribal queue task is open.
  • Set Review Type (CE / EA / EIS) before advancing — this stamps the target completion date.

Scoping

  • Issue the Notice of Intent (NOI) and open the public scoping period (minimum 30 days for EIS).
  • Identify cooperating agencies and confirm inter-agency coordination tasks are created.
  • Review the Litigation Risk Score — HIGH or VERY HIGH at Scoping warrants early legal coordination.
  • Check Challenge Prediction rules: any FIRED rules should drive proactive mitigation.
  • Set Project Sector and Circuit Court to enable sector-circuit risk scoring.

CE Determination

  • Confirm no Extraordinary Circumstances apply — if any apply, upgrade to EA.
  • CE page count must be 17 pages or fewer (P95 threshold) — above this, Timeline Risk Assessor flags At Risk.
  • Document the regulatory citation (40 CFR or agency CE list).
  • Confirm EJ/Tribal consultation complete if either proximity flag was set.

Environmental Assessment

  • Begin alternatives analysis and prepare the EA document.
  • EA page count threshold is 200 pages — above this, Timeline Risk Assessor flags At Risk automatically.
  • Verify Climate Assessment Required flag — if checked, complete climate analysis before advancing to Decision.
  • If EJ Analysis Required is set (NEPA Phase 2), document the analysis in the EA.

Draft EA Preparation

  • Finalize the draft EA document for public review.
  • Verify Defensibility Score — below 60 indicates documentation gaps that increase litigation risk.
  • Confirm all AI-drafted Decision Elements have SME Validation Date set before publication.
  • Ensure all public comments from any prior scoping are addressed in the draft.

Comment Period

  • Public comment period open — minimum 30 days for EA per CEQ rule.
  • All submitted comments are automatically classified by the AI Comment Classifier.
  • EJ/Tribal flagged comments route unconditionally to the specialist queue — check queue daily.
  • Comments with AI Confidence Score below 70 are escalated for human review automatically.
  • Monitor timeline risk tier — if it transitions to Stalled or Overdue, a coordinator task fires.

FONSI and Decision

  • Prepare Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI) if no significant impacts identified.
  • Defensibility Score must be 70 or higher for a well-defended FONSI — below this, address documentation gaps first.
  • Climate Assessment Complete must be checked if Climate Assessment Required is set (Phase 2 gate).
  • If significant impacts found, upgrade pathway to EIS — update Review Type accordingly.

Inter-Agency Coordination

  • Confirm Lead Agency and Cooperating Agency designations are documented.
  • Issue scoping notice — EIS scoping period minimum 30 days.
  • Tribal Lands Flag set: tribal consultation must be documented before advancing.
  • Scoping Baseline Months shows the empirical median for your agency — plan against this, not the statutory maximum.

Draft EIS Preparation

  • Prepare the Draft EIS — CEQ rule sets a 150-page normal / 300-page maximum limit.
  • Climate Assessment Required must be completed before advancing to Decision stage (Phase 2 gate).
  • Verify EJ Analysis Required — document analysis in Chapter 4 (Environmental Consequences).
  • Monitor Challenge Prediction rules — FIRED rules at this stage should inform alternatives analysis.
  • Defensibility Score below 60 indicates high documentation risk — review gap list in Risk Intelligence tab.

Draft EIS Comment Period

  • Minimum 45-day public comment period required for Draft EIS (CEQ rule).
  • All comments classified automatically — EJ/Tribal routed to specialist queue unconditionally.
  • Comments must be individually addressed in the Final EIS per 40 CFR 1503.4.
  • Monitor timeline risk — EIS Stalled status triggers escalation task automatically.

Final EIS Preparation

  • Respond to all substantive public comments from the Draft EIS period.
  • Verify all AI-drafted Decision Elements have Human Validated By set (OMB M-25-21 compliance).
  • Defensibility Score target 75 or higher before publishing Final EIS.
  • Plaintiff Risk Flag and Tribal Plaintiff Flag — both create legal coordination tasks automatically.

Record of Decision

  • ROD must be published no sooner than 30 days after Final EIS availability in the Federal Register.
  • Climate Assessment Complete must be checked before advancing to Decision (Phase 2 gate).
  • Administrative Record closes automatically when ROD outcome is set.
  • Defensibility Score of 70 or higher recommended — below this, document the basis explicitly.

Analysis

  • Environmental analysis and alternatives development underway.
  • Review Litigation Risk Score and Challenge Prediction rules — address FIRED rules now to reduce appeal risk.
  • Confirm any seasonal Work Order constraints have been scheduled (habitat surveys, etc.).
  • If EJ Analysis Required, ensure analysis is incorporated in this phase.

Review

  • Internal and cooperating agency review of draft documents.
  • Verify all AI-generated comment classifications have Human Validated By set before finalizing.
  • Defensibility Score should be monitored — drops indicate new documentation gaps.

Coordination

  • Action Plan for EA/EIS Coordination launched automatically at this stage.
  • Confirm cooperating agencies have accepted their coordination tasks.
  • Section 7 (ESA) and Section 106 (NHPA) consultations should be initiated if applicable.
  • Tribal Lands Flag set: tribal consultation must be resolved before advancing.

Determination

  • CE Action Plan launched automatically at this stage — confirm plan tasks are assigned.
  • If no Extraordinary Circumstances: document CE determination with regulatory citation.
  • If Extraordinary Circumstances found: upgrade pathway to EA before issuing any determination.
  • Confirm EJ/Tribal specialist consultation is complete before signing determination.

Decision

  • Final decision stage — FONSI, CE Determination, or ROD depending on review type.
  • Climate Assessment Complete is required at this stage if Climate Assessment Required is set (Phase 2 gate — system will block save if incomplete).
  • Defensibility Score — review the Risk Intelligence tab for all gap factors.
  • Administrative Record locks automatically when Decision outcome is recorded.

Decision Issued

  • Decision has been issued and published — NEPA process complete.
  • Confirm Administrative Record is locked (AR Locked = true).
  • Completion Date is stamped automatically when process status = completed.
  • If litigation is filed, create a Litigation record linked to this process.
